text: Comprehensive evaluation is one of the methods used to achieve the development of an institution. It helps us to determine the positive and negative features of the programs we carry out and enables us to take decisions with full knowledge of the situation. In this study, we explore the basic concepts, aims and principles of educational program evaluation and the steps to follow to conduct evaluation work efficiently. It makes a critical analysis on the current educational literature on the models and factors affecting the models of educational evaluation. The results of the research show that the purpose of the educational evaluation is to evaluate the success of the programs, to determine the areas that need improvement and to increase the educational quality of the programs carried out. In order to achieve the efficiency of the educational evaluation studies, it is important to maintain an objective stance from the planning stage to the implementation and control stage. It has been determined in the study that the government and institution regulations constitute an important supporting factor in the implementation of the programs, and that the programs are hindered by limited budgets and insufficient expertise. On the other hand, the study concludes by suggesting solutions to address the obstacles to the implementation of educational programs. It is stated that the success of the educational programs depends on the evaluation culture and the participation of the stakeholders.
explanation: The passage uses very generic, formulaic language with awkward phrasing (e.g., 'models affecting the models'), a hallmark of AI-generated or heavily paraphrased text. The absence of concrete details or citations makes it more likely to be machine-produced than a polished human-authored abstract.
